title: Authorization, Object Ownership, Properties, Functions, and Tenancy · Language Lens: Property Mass Assignment
summary: Authenticated users can enumerate another tenant’s records, mass-assign privileged fields, call admin actions through hidden routes, and infer denied objects from response differences. Planned artifact: Add and verify the httpsgo property mass assignment increment in deny-by-default authorization decision matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Allowlist readable and writable properties by operation and principal, reject server-owned fields, and prevent over-posting through explicit command types.
Load or constrain the target within the authorized tenant and ownership relationship before read, write, delete, export, or nested access.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Go server Predict Property Mass Assignment · Theory Property Mass Assignment
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory team handles case 4561 in a multi-tenant case service with a bounded PostgreSQL pool. Build deterministic Go evidence to enforce tenant, action, and property authorization as resource policy; then change the tenant and show deny-by-default before repository access. Browser code models decisions only. Listener, TLS, database, proxy, race, load, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Property Mass Assignment contract for the supplied data.
